mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or returns are negative in >50% of co-occurring months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the following week during economic expansions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months when industrial production increases during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the 260 index level triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in >60% of months when CPIAUCSL exceeds 260.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust economic activity and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in >55% of months with positive payroll growth over a 5-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and falling retail sales signals a synchronized demand contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in >50% of observed cases, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive months of falling industrial production confirm a manufacturing downturn that reduces corporate earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances following three consecutive months of industrial production declines.
